prompt: Edit image 1 (healthy fundus) by adding features similar to the exudates in image 2. Apply a heatmap overlay onto the fundus image, styled like image 3, highlighting areas of potential pathology, including the added exudates.
prompt_refined: Edit image 1, the healthy fundus, by introducing photorealistic, yellowish-white, irregular hard exudates, similar in appearance and distribution to those present in image 2. Position these exudates primarily in the central to central-right area of the fundus. Following this, apply a comprehensive heatmap overlay to the entire edited fundus image. The heatmap should emulate the color gradient style of image 3, transitioning from cool colors (blue/green) for healthy regions to warm colors (yellow/orange/red) for pathological areas. Crucially, the heatmap must highlight areas of potential pathology, with the highest intensity (red/orange) specifically focused on and encompassing the newly added exudates.
objects: Hard exudates in the central to central-right area of the fundus
Red/orange heatmap intensity focused on and encompassing the exudates
